6.def:
Definition

Partograph (Partogram): A graphic pictorial record of the progress of labour and the condition of the mother and fetus, plotted against time on a single standardised chart. Promoted by the WHO as a low-cost, evidence-based tool to detect prolonged or obstructed labour early and guide timely referral or intervention. Labour is plotted from 4 cm cervical dilatation; crossing the alert line signals slow progress and crossing the action line (4 hours later) mandates immediate action.
